reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for the positive feedback about working with us on the creation of your father’s trust. We’re happy to hear you felt working with the firm was efficient and everything worked smoothly.

We’re sorry that the estate administration process after your mother passed away didn’t go as smoothly as your first time working with the firm.

Estate administration is a complicated process of working with the Probate Court and a lot of it is largely out of our (and anyone’s) control.

When assisting our clients with complicated probate matters, we truly wish we wish we were able to tell them exactly what is in store. But we’re often at the mercy of both the process and the client’s own specific circumstances. A crystal ball sure would be a nice thing to have!

The delay you mentioned in your review was due to a mistake made by one of the Clerks at the Probate Court. Both the Attorney and the Paralegal explained that to you and clarified how that mistake was no fault of our own.

We recognize that you were displeased with how your estate administration experience went. We hope that any future experiences you may have with the Probate Court system go more smoothly.

Working with Nick Valinsky at Romano Parker Associates was a smooth and reassuring experience from start to finish. I came to him with a complex situation involving my home solar system, specifically, an issue where my solar production wasn't being properly tracked or routed due to a disconnect between Tesla Powerwall integration and a third-party ground mount system. The installer had used a side tap but didn’t fully coordinate the setup, which led to production losses and an incomplete view of system performance in the Tesla app.

From the beginning, Nick was professional, kind, and highly attentive. He patiently answered all of my questions and helped me navigate each step of the dispute. Though the resolution process involved some back-and-forth and required me to dig in and help coordinate with an independent electrician, Nick remained a steady presence. He ensured the process didn’t stall, kept communication open, and acted as a strong advocate when things needed to move forward.

In the end, we were able to secure reimbursement for all lost production and the out-of-pocket costs for the electrician and wiring correction—over $2500 total. There were some minor legal fees deducted, but considering the outcome and the fact that I avoided additional delays and headaches, it was well worth it.

I’m incredibly grateful for Nick’s support. Even when things got frustrating, he was always responsive and on point. If you're dealing with a complicated solar install issue or just want someone who will really have your back in any property-related dispute, Nick’s the person you want in your corner.
Thank you so much for helping me with the snow contract.

I was not happy with Attorney Robert Murphy's services. He was assigned to represent my father in a conservatorship case. He charged for each call he made, even when he could not connect with someone. The minimum charge was 1/10 of an hour. So, for example, while my father was in the hospital, Attny Murphy tried calling him several times, but my father was not always available to talk. He charged $27.50 for each call he placed when no connection was made. When he called me to gather information about my father's hospitalization, he did not disclose that he would be charging for the call, but that 15 minute call did also appear on my father's bill. He also spoke very slowly on the phone and in the hearing which just made dollar signs spin in my head watching the money go go go... I did express my discontent when my father received the bill. Attorney Murphy did not address all my billing questions, but did agree to lower the bill by 1/10. So that is why he is getting 2 stars instead of 1. But it makes me very uncomfortable that lawyers are able to do this type of billing legally, especially when dealing with a client with a mental illness.

I was lost in a world of shock, chaos and devastation when my son was unexpectedly taken from me by his father. I had joint custody with my son's father and allowed him to take my son on a vacation. I had no idea how much that seemingly innocent decision would devastate my life and rip my son from my world. Lies were told to the court in order for his father to gain an emergency Ex-Parte against me and take my son into his physical custody. The police couldn't help me! I was absolutely lost and devastated! I had never hired an attorney before so it was shocking to discover the common price range for family court attorneys was $7,500+. Its needless to say that as a single mom of two children, I quickly lost any semblance of hope because I didn't have that type of money. However, I couldn't allow a monster to take my son from me. I had to fight for him regardless of how daunting the task appeared. I contacted several attorneys but they either didn't want to accept my case due to a lack of $7000+ retainer upfront or they simply didn't call me back. I had (one week) until the court date to save my son and no representation. I found Mr. Brownstein's website highlighting his wide array of legal practice areas as well as his 20 years experience and I reached out to him. No sooner had I submitted a brief summary of my legal troubles to his website requesting a consultation....He called me! He called me within 5-10 minutes! I couldn't believe it! He is such a kind and caring man! He patiently listened to me rattle off my troubles on the phone, and then proceeded to explain his fee. I admit that I did cringe inwardly because, again, I didn't have access to thousands of dollars. I was very candid with him about it but he did not let that thwart his intentions to help me! I met with him soon after our initial telephone conversation and quickly got to work.
My court date was yesterday...and I'm extatically pleased to report that we won the case! Mr. Brownstein did a phenomenal job in court! 20 years of experience manifested before my eyes! He tore my son's father's defense team apart and left them unwilling to further argue against him! To say that attorney Brownstein is a great lawyer is an understatement! The man is brilliant! he is so very kind and sympathetic to his cases! He is Connecticut's best kept secret!
He is not just my lawyer, but now a very good friend! I would recommend him to anyone! He is 5 stars! respectful, calm, kind and very brilliant! If you think that all hope is lost regarding your court case....just call Jeffrey Brownstein! I'm glad I did!
Thank you Jeff!

As a result of a divorce settlement, my daughter was given title to the family house.  Unfortunately, the title transfer was tied up in an appeal.

While in appeal, her spouse failed to pay income taxes resulting in State and Federal tax liens on the property.   We contacted one of the more prominent tax relief services.  They were very encouraging, took our money, but failed to deliver.

We were about to give up when we decided to give it one more try.    Through a web search, we learned about O'Connor & Lyon, LLP.   Their web site said they had extensive experience in tax law.   They were also located in CT where my daughter lives.  The tax liens on the property were both State and Federal.

We called Attorney Lyons and discussed the situation.   He was very encouraging and thought we had a good case for tax relief.  Havng gone through this before, we were skeptical but decided to try again, this time retaining Attorney Lyons.

After gathering background information on the divorce settlement, IRS communications and the like, Attorney Lyons drafted a letter to the IRS requesting relief.   It was extremely well-written.   The letter went point-for-point through the timeline of the divorce, the tax debt, and the basis upon which the IRS should grant relief.   Although we did not know what would be the final outcome, our skepticism was lessened.   We felt Attorney Lyons had grasped the situation and made the best case possible for relief.

Our opinion was confirmed when the IRS approved full relief.  We could not be more pleased with the professionalism, expert tax knowledge, and speed with which Attorney Lyons handled this case.   The fees were most reasonable.   O'Connor and Lyons felt more like a concierge service rather than a run-of-the-mill tax relief service.  I would strongly recommend O'Connor and Lyons for anyone seeking tax relief.

Attorney Meghan Woods took my workman’s comp case, many months after I’d gotten another Attorney from a firm that is constantly advertising on radio and t.v. and of which I figured had to know what they were doing. It became evident to me that the first Firm and their lawyer were totally ineffectual and grossly mishandling my case after 6 months, to the extent they let an important Deposition languish until the respondent filed for discontinuation- because I had ignored a ‘notice of Deposition’ which was part two of a Deposition which began 2 months earlier. The first firm ignored the notice almost costing me the case by the time I received the notice from Worker’s Comp, with days to spare and all because the first Attorney and Firm did not act! My particular case was an incredibly hard case.
My employer,  despite nearly two decades of my  being a diligent, dependable hard worker- rewarding me every single year with written ‘top-notch’ reviews and raises- threw me under the bus after forcing me to do a work order that was physically impossible, and despite my admonition that I ‘did not need to get injured!’ The result? I was injured and am now permanently disabled. Over the next two years and 5 months and thats without counting the initial 6 months with the first  Attorney & Firm, Attorney Meghan Woods and Firm never gave up on me or my case! She and her Firm expertly out maneuvered the opposition representing my former employer, who I might add appeared to be waiting for my demise- thus making them ‘self-absolved’ financially-of my severe and permanent injury until finally a trial was requested by our side, and after many months a date was set. Well, just a little over a week before trial I received a call from Attorney Woods with a ‘Settlement Offer’.. a sizable 6 figure sum which Attorney Woods spent the next week negotiating and renegotiating to obtain a fair figure for me to consider, and which I accepted. I want to note that I spent countless hours on-line studying every aspect of Worker’s Comp per the State of Connecticut and it became apparent that Law Firm’s and Attorney’s who handle Workman’s Comp cases… are virtually some of the hardest working Attorneys in the Industry! And I discovered that my countless hours of reading, studying and saving everything I could find- on my computer to study further- meant very little as sometimes- ‘nothing is what it seems in Worker’s Comp!’ Don’t try to play Attorney. They know what they’re doing. Attorney Meghan Wood’s and the Firm were incredibly responsive, always patient with all my questions, which I occasionally sent via e-mail and unlike the first Firm, treated me like  my case totally mattered and was never ‘put on the back burner!’ Attorney Wood’s has a great personality, is sharp as can be, and most importantly…. While there are no guarantee’s of a case being won or settled, the Attorney/Firm that represents you is the most important aspect in a Worker’s Comp case, especially when they are very serious and complicated cases like mine! Before hiring Attorney Woods and her Firm, I’d met with other Firm’s and Lawyers who told me out right that I had a ‘very tough case’ ( that I believe they didn’t want to take on! ) If there was a 100 Star rating for any reviews, I would give Attorney Woods, Vargas & Chapman & all staff a 100++!

reviews.text: My fiance and I chose to use Aeton Law Partners (specifically, Wendy Estela) on referral from an attorney family member of ours residing in a different state. Since both of us were joining the same practice (we work in healthcare), one of us being the first specialist of their kind at the location, the hiring process was relatively extensive and involved several months of discussions, emails, and visits before we even saw an offer or contract. You could imagine that we were nervous to select the best individual to help us deliver on the investment. Wendy was not only extremely professional, but also knowledgeable, kind, responsive, and meticulous in advising us. She consistently caught important wording in our document that we had almost dismissed as minutia and brought up good points for general contractual concepts. As it happens, even the smallest changes made huge differences in how we better guaranteed a fair agreement with our new workplace. She spotted things that even the original contract authors found to be astute and worthy of attention/rewording. We appreciated her patience, efficiency, and flexibility in discussing each detail with us in ways that we could understand even though we were not in the field of law. The three of us decided together to avoid involving her in actual contract negotiation in order to encourage a continued positive relationship with our future colleagues, but we felt she prepared us well for things to bring up in the final discussions. We also felt that her price for both sets of contracts was more than fair. We would recommend Wendy and Aeton Law Partners to anyone making a big jump in their careers and wanting to ensure optimal fairness, longevity, and satisfaction with their contracts. Thanks, Wendy!

Carlson & Dumeer LLC did a fantastic job representing me to fight unjust violation.

Several friends and coworkers laughed at me for even looking for legal representation to fight a minor violation that consisted of a small fine with no potential for any jail time. The violation was unjust and I refused to give up and give in, but the only evidence was my word against that of the police. Demonstrating it was unjust in court seemed an impossible task; I knew I did not have the tools, wherewithal, and expertise to stand up for myself on my own.

That’s where Carlson & Dumeer’s team of experts - the attorneys, the paralegals, and the office staff - came in. They formulated a strategy, put together a plan, held me accountable for executing my parts of the plan, and did the seemingly impossible to win the case! In my specific circumstance I did not even have to show up in court myself; I was able to execute my parts remotely, and on my day in court I just had to remain available on the phone in case they called.

Who’s laughing now? In addition to not having to pay a fine to a state that already taxes us very heavily - too heavily for what we actually get out of it, in my opinion - winning this case meant I would be spared all associated downstream costs, saving me considerably over time. It also meant, importantly, that justice had finally been served.

Retaining quality legal counsel for important issues is always a good plan, and in my opinion Carlson & Dumeer is just that. The firm exceeded my expectations and I highly recommend them to anyone who needs quality expertise and representation.
